The pulmonary vasculature, however, is not congested and there is no evidence of acute or chronic pulmonary parenchymal infiltrates. The lateral and posterior pleural sinuses are free and no pneumothorax is identified in the apical area. Skeleton skeletal structures are grossly within normal limits. Because of patient's prominent ___, the area of the lower thoracic and upper lumbar spine cannot be identified with any detail. Our records include multiple previous chest examinations, most of which were taken in [DATE]. On these frontal chest examinations, there is no evidence of any compression fracture of any vertebral body.

 IMPRESSION:

 For the conclusive diagnosis of a fracture in this area, dedicated lumbar spine examination is recommended.
